posts.author: plinecom (Masataka Koike)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,

I read these two articles.

Add image/photo to a column (similar to People column) Feature requests
Hello, I can’t seem to find anything on the forums about this, but would be surprised if I am the first to inquire. Is there a way to add a photo to a column so that it appears just like the people column? For example, I would like to upload a profile photo or a logo for a client. At this time, I am adding them in the info boxes which you have to make a point to go into. I have seen on one of the tutorials where someone had an “Image” column and it was thumbnail photos of cupcakes or the li…
monday.com Blog – 29 Oct 20

More item height options, pie chart updates, and more!

Read all about monday.com's latest features, monday apps marketplace, upgrades, and more in our latest edition of our weekly Captain's Log!

Est. reading time: 3 minutes

Triple height is a great improvement. However, let me say one more thing.

You should add an option to make the margins of the image thumbnails as small as possible to fit into the frame while maintaining the aspect ratio, like in the attached image.

expand1729×1164 112 KB

Both Airtable and Google spreadsheet can do this, but not monday.com.
Since I rely on image thumbnails for my filmmaking projects, the thumbnails must look large in the list. So, please accept this proposed modification.

Thank you.

posts.author: ReturnOfTheMac (M-A C)
posts.cookedHtml:
I totally second that request. The thumbnails are too small even at maximum row height.
We have built boards with the file column but it’s not good UX, you need to hover to see something.
Our current workaround which it not ideal is to use an aditionnal card view that doesn’t give us the information we like in a regular board view.

If you agree and would benefit from bigger thumbnails please consider voting for this post (at the top left of this thread!)
Thank you

posts.author: EricBarker (Eric Barker)
posts.cookedHtml: This is a MAJOR issues for my company (Video Production). We Include a thumbnail for each of our YouTube projects for easy identification. We came from a giant Google Sheet, which displayed this very nicely but was otherwise far less sophisticated than Monday. It’s been the one sticking point for selling Monday to my boss, as he’s a very visual worker. We convinced him to do it anyway, but it’s been an ongoing request! I suspect that the “attachments” column wasn’t exactly designed for images in mind, so maybe we need a separate “Thumbnails” or “Images” column type.
